keyEvents.event: Victory in the Great Patriotic War
keyEvents.impact: Core legitimating myth; willingness to absorb enormous costs in existential conflict
Established nuclear parity as the foundation of great power status
Framed by leadership as the greatest geopolitical catastrophe of the century; revanchist drive to restore lost sphere
Deep distrust of Western economic prescriptions; preference for state-controlled strategic sectors

keyEvents.event: NATO intervention in Kosovo and eastward expansion
keyEvents.impact: Conviction that NATO enlargement is an existential encirclement threat
Demonstrated willingness to redraw borders by force and absorb sanctions for territorial gains
Open confrontation with the West; war economy, isolation from Europe, and pivot to China

history.narrative: A wounded great power that equates security with buffer zones and respect with fear. Highly risk-acceptant, skilled at hybrid warfare and intelligence operations, and willing to endure isolation and economic pain to reverse the post-Cold War settlement.

redLines.description: NATO expansion into the post-Soviet sphere or NATO forces near Russian borders
Military threats to Crimea, Kaliningrad, or claimed annexed territories
Western-backed color revolutions in Russia or allied states
Conventional strikes threatening strategic nuclear forces or early-warning systems

goals.description: Restore dominance over the former Soviet space, starting with Ukraine and Belarus
Roll back NATO presence in Eastern Europe and split the Atlantic alliance
Force recognition as an indispensable great power in a multipolar order
Evade Western sanctions via energy leverage and trade pivots to China, India, and the Gulf
Undermine Western liberal democracies through disinformation and election interference
